_description: Expected post-registration state for the CSI-01 self-registration mechanism. Used by the Phase 1a fast-lane verifier to compare actual vs. expected state after the session-start hook fires for the first time. The Pattern A paired oracle is the existing `dispatchable-sessions` oracle reading the real registry — drift between this fixture's `expectedRegistry` shape and what the oracle returns surfaces a CSI-01 regression.

scenarios.name: interactive-session-fresh
scenarios.description: An interactive Claude session opens in a project with NO pre-existing registry entry. The session-start hook is the only writer. Result: registry has exactly one entry; this-session.json mirrors it.

scenarios.name: spawn-script-then-hook-confirm
scenarios.description: Spawn-script wrote the entry pre-launch (DEC-24 optimization path). The session-start hook reads CLAUDE_SESSION_ID from env, finds the existing entry by id, and confirms-replaces it with the canonical shape (refreshed claudeShellPid + claudeShellPidWrittenAt). spawnedAt and descriptive fields (role, podsWindow, technology, tasks) MUST survive the merge.
invariants: expectedRegistry.sessionsAt0.role preserved from input.preExistingEntry.role (descriptive field NOT overwritten by hook)
expectedRegistry.sessionsAt0.spawnedAt preserved from input.preExistingEntry.spawnedAt (NOT overwritten with hook's 'now')

Sample literal values shown in `input.preExistingEntry` are illustrative -- the contract is shape-level (matched via <MATCH:> patterns) and preservation-level (asserted by the smoke harness's literal-value assertions on the same registry, not duplicated here).

scenarios.name: re-invocation-stable-identity
scenarios.description: Hook fires a second time in the same Claude shell (e.g., after compaction). this-session.json already exists. The hook reads it, leaves sessionId/sessionRole/createdAt/workDir unchanged, only refreshes claudeShellPid + claudeShellPidWrittenAt in the registry.
invariants: expectedSessionState.sessionId stable across re-invocation (identity preserved -- asserted by the smoke harness, not by this fixture)
expectedSessionState.createdAt stable across re-invocation (NOT refreshed -- asserted by the smoke harness)
Sample literal values in `input.preExistingEntry` are illustrative; the fixture asserts shape only. Cross-invocation invariants are asserted by csi-01-smoke.{sh,ps1}.

matchSyntax._doc: Fields whose values begin with '<MATCH:' are matched as Python-flavoured regular expressions against the actual value's string form. Fields without the '<MATCH:' prefix are matched literally. Lists are order-sensitive unless wrapped in 'sessionsContainsExactlyOneWith' or similar predicate keys.

pairedOracle.name: dispatchable-sessions
pairedOracle._doc: The existing dispatchable-sessions oracle reads the real registry. Drift between this fixture's expectedRegistry shape and what the oracle returns indicates a CSI-01 regression. The fixture is the static expectation; the oracle is the live-state mirror. Validation: invoke the oracle after a fresh session start and compare its output's `peers[].sessionRole` and `.id` against this fixture's matching scenario.
